stem: Bacteria are growing in a Petri dish. There were 200,000 cells per milliliter at the start of an observation. The number of cells per milliliter doubles every 4 hours. How many cells per milliliter will there be 20 hours after the start of the observation?
rationale: Choice C is correct. Let y represent the number of cells per milliliter x hours after the start. Because the number doubles every 4 hours, y = 200,000·2x/4. Substituting x = 20 gives y = 200,000·25 = 200,000·32 = 6,400,000. Choices A, B, and D arise from miscounting the number of doublings or arithmetic errors.
